run_type: scheduled daily (Day +4 post-migration; Day 8 of Nhughes09 author-commit drought)
headline: Day +4 post-migration. Three operator-hand items (GSC Domain property, Bing Webmaster, repo homepage field) remain unchanged from 2026-04-24. ONE notable new signal: a `site:funwithscience.net` Google query now returns exactly one stale legacy result (a 'Wimshurst Machine' product page from a prior tenant of the domain, now HTTP 404). This is a domain-history artifact — the domain previously hosted an unrelated 'Fun With Science Education Products' commercial site and Google still has that URL in its index even though it no longer resolves. None of OUR pages appear in any indexed search yet. Dome side: machine-readable layer fingerprints byte-identical to baseline for the 8th consecutive day; zero author-authored commits since 2026-04-17; only scheduled-bot churn (auto-update ECM status hourly, ECM data pull, Groq daily AI review). No third-party mention, no review citation, no claim overlap from prior runs. Content layer (docs/, data/) clean — no drift from yesterday's verified state.

operator_action_checklist.action: Verify funwithscience.net as a Google Search Console Domain property (DNS TXT via GoDaddy)
operator_action_checklist.rationale: Day +4 without verification. Today's site:funwithscience.net result confirms Google has SOME indexing history for the apex domain (a stale entry from the prior tenant), which means the domain is not crawler-cold — but that signal is not under our control until we own the GSC property. Verification unlocks: (a) sitemap submission, (b) URL Inspection / Index Now requests, (c) URL Removal requests for the stale legacy URLs. The stale-legacy finding strengthens, rather than weakens, the case for getting verification done now — a clean GSC property is the only path to flush the prior tenant's URLs from results.
operator_action_checklist.detected_signal: ls docs/google*.html -> no match; grep 'google-site-verification' docs/index.html -> no match. Unchanged since 2026-04-21 run2.
sub_actions_after_verify: Submit https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/sitemap.xml as a sitemap in the new property
URL Inspection push for https://funwithscience.net/ and https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/

Optional: file URL Removal for stale legacy URLs (e.g. https://funwithscience.net/product/wimshurst-machine/) — they 404, so they will eventually drop on their own, but a removal request accelerates the cleanup.

operator_action_checklist.action: Bing Webmaster Tools verification on funwithscience.net
operator_action_checklist.rationale: DNS TXT retry was scheduled for 2026-04-22 per SOCIAL-NOTE-004 item 2 — now 3 days past the retry window. No BingSiteAuth.xml and no msvalidate meta tag have landed. Secondary blocker behind GSC.

operator_action_checklist.fallback_if_dns_retry_fails_again: Social can draft an HTML meta verification patch (msvalidate.01) for the decider to wire into build-scripts/generate-html.js. Not needed yet — awaiting operator result on the DNS retry path.

operator_action_checklist.rationale: Public API confirms homepage still returns 'https://funwithscience-org.github.io/dome-model-review/' as of 2026-04-25 ~10:08 UTC. Cheap one-line fix; helps GitHub-repo-page SEO and resolves a live mismatch against every other canonical URL in the review's infrastructure.
operator_action_checklist.alternative: GitHub About panel edit in the repo web UI.

operator_action_checklist.action: Validate ClaimReview JSON-LD at validator.schema.org against the new canonical URL
operator_action_checklist.rationale: Schema structure unchanged through migration; url and author.url correctly reference funwithscience.net, itemReviewed.url correctly retains john09289.github.io/predictions. Low-risk sanity check still outstanding.
operator_action_checklist.detected_signal: No validator-reported errors observed locally; operator has not yet confirmed a live validator pass on the new canonical. No action from social — interactive human check.

author_activity_search_notes: Web search surface remains null. Queries run this run: '"john09289" OR "Nhughes09" dome firmament predictions 2026', '"ovoid cavity cosmological model" OR "ECM dome model" 2026', '"john09289.github.io/predictions" dome OR cosmology OR ECM', '"firmament resonance" "Schumann" cavity dome cosmology', '"dome model review" critique flat earth predictions'. Results: zero on-topic hits for the author or the model's distinguishing vocabulary. Adjacent firmament/dome communities (animaframework.com, journalofgeocentriccosmology.org, FlatEarth.ws, Walter Bislins) appear in generic-term searches but use entirely different technical vocabulary (no Finsler coordinates, no ovoid cavity, no 20015/8537/5733 km parameters, no 67/69-prediction taxonomy, no attribution to john09289). 8 days into the Nhughes09 author-commit drought and the external posting surface remains entirely empty — strengthens prior reading that the 2026-04-17 burst was a one-off AI-steering investment, not the start of an external-promotion phase.
claim_overlap_search_notes: No new significant technical overlap detected. ANIMA Framework, Journal of Geocentric Cosmology, and the WordPress 'Unified Dimensional Plasma Dome Layers' post all remain parallel-but-unrelated cosmologies — they share generic firmament/dome concepts but none reproduce the dome's distinguishing features (ovoid-cavity geometry, Finsler coordinates, exponential firmament profile H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619), 20015-km disc radius, 67/69-prediction taxonomy). No mirror sites, no forks, no translations.

The most recent direct authored commit is still a57e731 found via the human-readable commit log, verified independently here by inspecting the latest 10 commits and confirming all are scheduled-bot accounts.

repo_metadata.note: Unchanged — dome author still has not added any repo-level SEO metadata. 8+ days since the 2026-04-17 infrastructure burst.

machine_readable_fingerprints.note: All four fingerprints byte-identical to 2026-04-24 baseline. Eight consecutive days of zero author-authored changes to the machine-readable layer.

dome_repo.notable: Day 8 of the no-author-commit pattern. Hourly scheduled-bot churn continues but produces no semantic change — status JSON timestamps and daily_review.json regeneration only. No new infrastructure to counter.

repo_metadata.description: Scientific critical review of the Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model (ECM) — every claim checked against published data

review_mentions_search_notes: Explicit '"funwithscience.net" dome review' and '"funwithscience.net/dome-model-review"' queries return zero on-topic hits. No third-party reference to our review in any indexed source. Pre-indexing confirmed for our content.

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Day +4 of the post-migration window. Both sites still functionally unindexed for their own content. Our position remains structurally stronger than the dome's (brandable URL on funwithscience.net, ClaimReview JSON-LD, populated repo description+topics, honest llms.txt, accurate counts). Dome side has been static on author-authored infrastructure for 8 days — they have not added new AI-steering machinery for us to counter and have not addressed their own discoverability gaps (null repo description, null homepage, no topics). The new signal this run is the stale-legacy `site:funwithscience.net` result — Google has prior-tenant content (Wimshurst Machine product page, now 404) cached for our apex domain. This is a domain-history artifact, not a problem we caused, and it argues for accelerating GSC verification so we can request URL removals for the legacy entries. Counter-argument (per user preference): the legacy URL 404s, so Google will eventually drop it without our intervention — chasing a URL Removal request is low-leverage activity that becomes important only once we have indexed content of our own to push forward. The asymmetric work today is still GSC verification first; URL Removal is a downstream tactic.

actions_taken.action: Appended 2026-04-25 run 1 to monitor/social/search-rankings.json (same 11-query panel as 2026-04-24 for trend continuity, with one notable change: site:funwithscience.net now returns 1 result vs 0 yesterday).
actions_taken.reasoning: Day +4 indexing check. The single new ranking signal — Google has the prior tenant's URL indexed at our apex domain — is a real shift worth recording even though it's not OUR content.

actions_taken.action: Re-verified all five autodetect signals (GSC, Bing, IndexNow, GitHub homepage, meta-tag verification). All five remain pending/unchanged from 2026-04-24. Did NOT re-flag any as a new issue — flagged only the three specific items remaining (GSC, Bing, homepage) plus the optional schema validator check.
actions_taken.reasoning: Per SOCIAL-NOTE-003 directive, only flag items that are actually pending. Avoids the pre-2026-04-15 generic 'indexing strategy pending' phrasing that caused coordination gaps.

actions_taken.action: Re-verified our docs/llms.txt against current data: wins.json entries=71, uncounted-failures.json entries=10, dome_claimed_accuracy=94.5%, dome_claimed_failures=4, dome_claimed_prospective_count=10. Our llms.txt correctly states '69 dome claims (V51.1) plus additional sub-entries' (matches baseWins=69 + 2 sub-entries 057b/058b) and the ClaimReview JSON-LD correctly says 'has 69 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy' (the dome's own claim — what ClaimReview is supposed to encode). No drafts needed this run.
actions_taken.reasoning: Phase 1 ownership rule: social reads but does not write docs/. Drafts only emitted when content drifts from data/. Nothing drifted.

actions_taken.action: Re-verified the 2b1 formula tokens still resolve to 10/83.1% in the built HTML. grep '85.2' docs/index.html -> 0 matches; grep '83.1' docs/index.html -> 1 match. Template tokens {{UNCOUNTED_FAILURE_COUNT}} and {{INCL_FAILURES_ACCURACY_PCT}} present in data/sections.json. Closed loop holds across yesterday's content-layer commits.
actions_taken.reasoning: Sanity check the historical formula-drift fix is durable across multiple decider runs.

actions_taken.action: Re-fetched and md5'd dome machine-readable layer (llms.txt, ai_manifest.json, sitemap.xml, robots.txt). All four fingerprints byte-identical to 2026-04-24 baseline. Eighth consecutive day of dome stasis on author-authored infrastructure.
actions_taken.file: monitor/social/discoverability-baseline.json (read-only this run; no append needed since values unchanged)
actions_taken.reasoning: Confirms no new dome infrastructure to counter. The author has had 8 days to react to anything from us or anyone else and has chosen not to.

actions_taken.action: Logged a new finding: site:funwithscience.net now returns 1 result — a stale legacy URL ('Wimshurst Machine | Fun With Science Education Products and Teacher Supplies' at funwithscience.net/product/wimshurst-machine/, HTTP 404). This is from a prior tenant of the domain. Recorded in this report and in the rankings panel; added a sub-action to operator checklist item 1 (URL Removal once GSC is verified). Did NOT file a separate decider issue — the URL 404s and will fade naturally; chasing URL Removal before GSC verification is low-leverage.
actions_taken.reasoning: Strategic tracking: the prior tenant's index footprint partially explains why Google has any awareness of funwithscience.net at all, and forecasts that URL Removal will be a useful post-GSC-verification cleanup task.

discoverability.issues_filed_note: No new issues filed this run. All four operator-hand items from 2026-04-24 remain open in checklist form (not decider-routable because they require human auth or external service interaction). No new build-pipeline gaps detected. No drift in docs/ content relative to data/ sources. Dedup check against monitor/decisions/open-issues.json and closed-issues.json — no social-owned issues need re-filing or repair.

no_action_gaps.gap: Stale legacy URL (funwithscience.net/product/wimshurst-machine/) indexed at Google from a prior tenant of the domain.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: The URL returns HTTP 404. Google will deindex it on its own once enough recrawls confirm the 404 status. URL Removal via GSC is a faster cleanup path but it requires GSC Domain property verification first — which is already on the operator-hand checklist as item 1. Not worth filing a separate item; will be a natural sub-action once GSC is up. Logging here so we don't forget the cleanup opportunity.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has ai_manifest.json with ai_prompt_seed, agent_rules, and recommended_read_order designed to steer AI responses toward dome-favorable framings.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: AI-steering content (prompt-injection-adjacent patterns) is NOT something we should replicate. Our countermove is an honest, accurate, descriptive llms.txt that contradicts the dome's claims with verifiable evidence — not a counter-prompt-injection. We already have this. Continuing to not-match this pattern is correct.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has a /api/ JSON endpoint (current/status, current/methodology, current/claim_index) that gives AI systems structured access to their model.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Per prior reports: a custom /api/ path duplicates effort without a known consumer. ClaimReview JSON-LD embedded in the HTML is the W3C standard for fact-check structured data and is what fact-check aggregators actually consume. Hold.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome site git-commits hourly (100+ commits/24h) via auto-update bots, signaling 'active project' to crawlers that weight update frequency.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Matching this dilutes signal-to-noise. Our commit pattern (decider/analyst/curmudgeon on actual content) is what an honest project looks like. Declining to match remains correct.

docs/index.html ClaimReview JSON-LD (url and author.url on funwithscience.net; itemReviewed.url correctly retains john09289.github.io/predictions; claimReviewed='69 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy' matches dome's headline)
docs/index.html meta description and og: tags (count 69 matches dome_claimed; description accurately summarizes review scope)
docs/index.html canonical link rel='canonical' to funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/
data/sections.json part2b formula (template tokens {{UNCOUNTED_FAILURE_COUNT}} and {{INCL_FAILURES_ACCURACY_PCT}} present; renders 10 / 83.1%)
Apex landing page (https://funwithscience.net/) returns 200 with our description meta='Rigorous, data-first critiques of fringe scientific and pseudoscientific claims.'

summary_of_strategic_position.our_advantage: Clean infrastructure, honest content, populated metadata, brandable URL, structural ClaimReview — all Phase-1-complete and verified again today. Discoverability ceiling is capped by operator-hand items, not anything social or the build pipeline can address.
summary_of_strategic_position.dome_advantage: None that matters for indexed discoverability. Eight days of zero author-authored changes on the AI-steering layer mean any first-mover advantage they had is decaying — they invested on 2026-04-17 and have not iterated. Their lack of repo metadata and the absence of any sitemap entry past 2026-04-17 is a structural weakness.
summary_of_strategic_position.contrarian_read: Steelman the dome author's strategy: he may genuinely not care about external discoverability — his audience is whoever finds the GitHub Pages URL through TikTok or word-of-mouth, and he is investing in MAKING the on-page experience richer (live monitor, daily AI review, methodology page) rather than in EXTERNAL surfacing. Under that read, 8 days of no-iteration on llms.txt/sitemap/repo metadata isn't 'apathy', it's 'these are not the levers I'm pulling'. We should not assume our discoverability metrics are HIS metrics. Our equivalent contrarian risk: we are over-investing in being findable when nobody is looking for us yet — the audience for a dome-model critique is downstream of someone first encountering the dome model, and we have no upstream visibility there.
what_would_change_the_picture: Operator completes GSC Domain property verification -> indexing timeline starts and URL Removal becomes available for stale legacy URLs
Author resumes dev after the 8-day hiatus -> new dome infrastructure to analyze
Any third-party picks up the dome model -> claim overlap or review mentions to track
Someone forks the dome repo -> first signal of actual external interest
Stale legacy `site:funwithscience.net` URL deindexes -> first sign Google is recrawling our apex
